title: Tissue Collection and Extractions for RNA-seq
author: David Lowry
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.basiiece

steps.instruction: Pour liquid nitrogen into a polystyrene cooler or another container that can safely hold liquid nitrogen.
Remove tissue from plant and place in a tube. Make sure to clean scissors or foreceps with ethanol prior to collections.
Immediately plunge tube into liquid nitrogen (transcription profiles change rapidly, so do this as fast as possible).
Transfer tubes into a cooler containing dry ice for transport to a -80C freezer. Store at -80C until ready to extract.

Extract tissue with a Spectrum Plant Total RNA Kit (Sigma-Aldrich). The Lowry lab has achieved consistently high yields of RNA from Mimulus guttatus using this kit. https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/catalog/product/sigma/strn50?lang=en&region=US&gclid=CjwKCAiA__HvBRACEiwAbViuU4EOr5CpJNBCqRf-923Uw8ZrDfSQ6bRXNHIhX4t6lKE7jA5AAHO5UxoCtP0QAvD_BwE
